summary:
Fixed the “duplicate apply after snapshot-install + restart” bug.

Root cause  
InstallSnapshot restored the state machine and advanced lastApplied, but left commitIndex untouched.  
If the follower had never received the corresponding commit messages, commitIndex could be behind (often 0).  
After a crash/restart, recovery derives lastApplied from commitIndex, undoing the snapshot progress and causing
the same log entries to be applied again when a new leader replicates.

Fix (snapshot.go)  
After restoring from the snapshot:

    // ensure commitIndex ≥ snapshot boundary
    n.lastApplied = snap.LastIncludedIndex
    if n.commitIndex < snap.LastIncludedIndex {
        n.commitIndex = snap.LastIncludedIndex
    }

This preserves the Raft invariant lastApplied ≤ commitIndex and guarantees the
snapshot’s state is treated as committed across crashes.

test.sh now passes:

=== Raft Snapshot Commit-Gap Test ===
Building...
--- Round 1 --- … OK  
--- Round 2 --- … OK  
--- Round 3 --- … OK  
PASS: No duplicate state machine transitions across all rounds
